(born December 8, 1955) is an American musician who first emerged as the drummer of the [punk rock](/wiki/Punk_rock "Punk rock") band the Eyes (also featuring [Charlotte Caffey](/wiki/Charlotte_Caffey "Charlotte Caffey") of [the Go-Go's](/wiki/The_Go-Go%27s "The Go-Go's")). From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

(Redirected from [DJ Bonebrake](/w/index.php?title=DJ_Bonebrake&redirect=no "DJ Bonebrake"))

American drummer

| D. J. Bonebrake | |
| --- | --- |
| [Bonebrake performing with the Knitters in 2007](/wiki/File:D._J._Bonebrake.jpg "Bonebrake performing with the Knitters in 2007") Bonebrake performing with the Knitters in 2007 | |
| Background information | |
| Born | (1955-12-08) December 8, 1955 (age 70) |
| Genres | [Punk rock](/wiki/Punk_rock "Punk rock"), [roots rock](/wiki/Roots_rock "Roots rock"), [alternative country](/wiki/Alternative_country "Alternative country") |
| Instruments | Drums, percussion |
| Years active | 1977–present |
|  | |
| Website | [djbonebrakemusic.com](http://djbonebrakemusic.com) |

Musical artist

**Donald J. Bonebrake** (born December 8, 1955) is an American musician who first emerged as the drummer of the [punk rock](/wiki/Punk_rock "Punk rock") band the Eyes (also featuring [Charlotte Caffey](/wiki/Charlotte_Caffey "Charlotte Caffey") of [the Go-Go's](/wiki/The_Go-Go%27s "The Go-Go's")). He is best known as an original member of and drummer for punk band [X](/wiki/X_(American_band) "X (American band)"), of which he is still an active member. [[1]](#cite_note-1)

## Career

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=D._J._Bonebrake&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Career")]

Bonebrake, born in [Burbank, California](/wiki/Burbank,_California "Burbank, California"), and having spent his youth in the [San Fernando Valley](/wiki/San_Fernando_Valley "San Fernando Valley"), is the only founding member of X from California (the other three are from [Illinois](/wiki/Illinois "Illinois")).[[2]](#cite_note-2) Bonebrake also performed with two of the band's side projects: the [country](/wiki/Country_music "Country music")/[folk music](/wiki/Folk_music "Folk music")/punk band [the Knitters](/wiki/The_Knitters "The Knitters") (with his bandmates [John Doe](/wiki/John_Doe_(musician) "John Doe (musician)") and [Exene Cervenka](/wiki/Exene_Cervenka "Exene Cervenka")), and [Auntie Christ](/wiki/Auntie_Christ "Auntie Christ") (with Cervenka).

While a member of X, Bonebrake briefly guested as the drummer for [the Germs](/wiki/Germs_(band) "Germs (band)"), and during 1981, he and Doe served as members of [the Flesh Eaters](/wiki/The_Flesh_Eaters "The Flesh Eaters"), performing on that band's second album, *[A Minute to Pray, a Second to Die](/wiki/A_Minute_to_Pray,_a_Second_To_Die_(album) "A Minute to Pray, a Second To Die (album)")*.[[3]](#cite_note-3)

In 2010, Bonebrake joined the [Rancid](/wiki/Rancid_(band) "Rancid (band)") side project [Devil's Brigade](/wiki/Devil%27s_Brigade_(band) "Devil's Brigade (band)").[[4]](#cite_note-4)

In 2013, Bonebrake joined the World Takes, helmed by Stephen Maglio. The band released an album, *Love Songs for eX's*, and toured with the [Meat Puppets](/wiki/Meat_Puppets "Meat Puppets").

In 2021, [Dave Grohl](/wiki/Dave_Grohl "Dave Grohl") revealed that he and Bonebrake are cousins through Grohl's grandmother.[[5]](#cite_note-5)

Although most of his work has been within the punk genre, Bonebrake heads two jazz ensembles: the Bonebrake Syncopators, who play early [jazz standards](/wiki/Jazz_standards "Jazz standards") in a [swing](/wiki/Swing_music "Swing music") and [western swing](/wiki/Western_swing "Western swing") style; and Orchestra Superstring who play [Afro-Cuban jazz](/wiki/Afro-Cuban_jazz "Afro-Cuban jazz") and [Latin jazz](/wiki/Latin_jazz "Latin jazz"). In both jazz groups, Bonebrake plays [vibraphone](/wiki/Vibraphone "Vibraphone") or [marimba](/wiki/Marimba "Marimba") rather than a drum kit. Bonebrake has also performed as timpanist with the Palisades Symphony.[[6]](#cite_note-6) His marimba playing goes back at least as far as his first work with the Flesh Eaters.[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*]
